channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Chinese scientists ran an AI program on a virtual light-based computer system inside its real 'digital twin' PC — you can't get more meta than that (thanks Inception)
item.description: Chinese researchers created a digital twin optical computing framework allowing AI training, optimization, and validation before deployment on hardware.

item.title: Test du Samsung Galaxy S26 Plus : le smartphone qui ne devait pas exister ?
item.description: J'ai testé le Galaxy S26 Plus et j'ai l'impression qu'il n'était pas vraiment prévu au programme.

item.title: In the Weights is your new AI-centric vanity search
item.description: So ... what's your In the Weights score?

item.title: Microsoft CSO acknowledges that humans are struggling to keep up with AI advancement, reckons we've got a 'narrowing window to understand AI' before it's, well, too late
item.description: Researchers warn AI capabilities are advancing faster than human understanding, creating growing concerns about oversight, transparency, and control.

item.title: The upcoming Star Fox 64 remake is too conservative, but wouldn’t you overcorrect after a disaster like Star Fox Zero?
item.description: We return to the wondrous world of Star Fox Zero on the Wii U ahead of the launch of the next Star Fox 64 remake.

item.title: NASA is testing a rover that can drive faster and lift its wheels to climb obstacles
item.description: The space agency shared footage of tests with its Ernest prototype rover.

item.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
item.description: Atlantic reporter Alex Reisner recently uncovered four datasets of music being used to train AI models and made them fully searchable for the public. Two of the sets are absolutely enormous at 12 million and 9 million tracks. The other two are much smaller, but still represent a significant amount of training data at over […]

item.title: Founders Fund’s outlier bet on humanely killed fish
item.description: Shinkei makes a refrigerator-sized robot called Poseidon to kill fish quickly and humanely.
